terms.definition: Matplotlib's state-based interface (imported as plt) providing functions like plot, scatter, xlabel, and show that operate on the current figure and axes.
Top-level Matplotlib container for one or more Axes; controls overall canvas size (figsize) and file output via savefig.
A single plot region within a Figure; the object where scatter, plot, hist, bar, set_xlabel, and legend are called in OO style.
Function plt.subplots(rows, cols) that creates a Figure and a grid of Axes arrays for multi-panel layouts.
Any drawable Matplotlib element—lines, markers, text, patches—attached to an Axes or Figure.
Figure method that writes the current canvas to a file (PNG, PDF, SVG) with optional dpi and bbox_inches parameters.
